selected_title: Polity, Practice and the Mission of the United Methodist Church
question: Which measurable boundary in “Polity, Practice and the Mission of the United Methodist Church” changes how Polity should be evaluated after “Centralization and decentralization reform in school education in Nepal: Tracing the swing of the pendulum”?

evidence_spans.text: In this in-depth analysis of the connection between United Methodist polity and theology, Thomas E. Frank addresses the ways in which historical developments have shaped -- and continue to shape -- the organization of the church. The author discusses emerging issues that must be faced by the denomination in the present and near future, and indicates how these issues come to bear upon concrete polity matters. Including all recent changes adopted by the 1996 General Conference, this volume is keyed to the new Book of Discipline and provides polity classes with a text that moves beyond a narrow analysis of rules and regulations.

learned_claims: The inspected source addresses Polity, Political science through the evidence summarized in the preserved span.
The abstract supplies a direct evidence span of 103 words; interpretation remains bounded to that span.
